skill.name: writing-assistant
skill.description: Guides users from unclear ideas to structured outlines and finished articles through staged diagnosis, idea mining, topic selection, framework building, and drafting.
skill.summary: Structured writing assistant that adapts the workflow to topic clarity.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 22 potential issues, including external command, weak cryptography, and obfuscation heuristics. Manual review found these are false positives caused by markdown fences, inline file names, front matter text, and Chinese markdown prose. No evidence found of executable code, network access, environment access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are markdown code fences, inline markdown file paths, or instructions to create user-facing draft files. They do not contain Ruby code, shell execution, or executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines were inspected and are markdown flowcharts, table entries, fenced templates, or plain file naming instructions. No command interpreter, dynamic execution, or shell syntax is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Text Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alert points to the skill front matter description. It contains Chinese natural language describing the writing workflow, not a cryptographic algorithm or hash function.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is a YAML description field with no cryptographic code or algorithm reference. This is a clear scanner artifact.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Chinese Markdown Prose Misclassified as Obfuscation
low_findings.description: The entropy alerts point to markdown files written in Chinese with formatting characters. Review found readable instructions, not encoded payloads, encrypted blobs, or binary content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All reviewed files are legible markdown stage instructions. No evidence found of packed, encoded, or encrypted content.

content.value_statement: Many writers have useful thoughts but cannot turn them into a clear article. This skill guides topic diagnosis, idea mining, outlining, drafting, and revision in one staged workflow.
actual_capabilities: Diagnoses whether a writing idea is clear or still vague
Guides idea mining when the user has scattered thoughts
Helps select a focused topic and one-sentence core message
Builds article frameworks with audience value and section balance
Drafts articles from an approved outline while preserving user tone
Supports iterative revision based on user feedback
limitations: Does not perform external research or fact checking
Requires user input to confirm topic direction and outline quality
Focuses on article-style writing rather than citations or academic formatting
May need several rounds for complex or unclear subjects

use_cases.title: Turn Scattered Ideas Into an Article
use_cases.description: Use the staged mining path to capture thoughts, find the core message, build an outline, and draft a coherent article.

use_cases.title: Develop a Clear Article Framework
use_cases.description: Start from a known thesis and refine audience, logic, section order, examples, and word count before drafting.
use_cases.target_user: Professionals preparing essays or thought pieces

use_cases.title: Preserve Voice During Drafting
use_cases.description: Use collected phrases and user examples to produce a draft that keeps the writer's original tone.
use_cases.target_user: Creators who want AI help without losing personal style

prompt_templates.title: Start With a Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to write about {topic}. Please help me decide whether my idea is clear enough to outline.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request when the topic is broad or new

prompt_templates.title: Mine Raw Thoughts
prompt_templates.prompt: I have scattered thoughts about {topic}. Ask me questions and help me record useful insights.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when ideas exist but the core message is unclear

prompt_templates.title: Build the Framework
prompt_templates.prompt: My core message is {message}. Help me build an article framework for {audience} with clear reader value.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after the thesis is mostly clear

prompt_templates.title: Draft From an Approved Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this outline: {outline}. Write a {word_count} article in my {tone} style and keep the logic tight.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request when structure, audience, and tone are defined

output_examples.input: I want to write about why teams should document decisions, but my thoughts are messy.
output_examples.output: The skill would first run idea mining, capture the strongest examples, identify one core message, then propose an outline with reader problem, decision cost, documentation method, and closing takeaway.

output_examples.input: My thesis is clear: remote teams need written norms. Help me write the article.
output_examples.output: The skill would skip broad mining, confirm the audience and reader value, build a concise framework, then draft the article from the approved structure.

output_examples.input: The draft feels too formal and does not sound like me.
output_examples.output: The skill would revise the draft by shortening sentences, restoring user phrases, adding concrete examples, and checking whether each section still supports the core message.

best_practices: Share real examples, phrases, and scenarios during the idea mining stage.
Confirm the core message before moving into framework building.
Review the outline carefully before asking for a full draft.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a full draft before the topic and reader value are clear.
Do not skip user confirmation when several possible messages compete.
Do not provide only abstract themes without examples or target readers.

faq.question: What type of writing does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports article-style writing, essays, thought pieces, and reflective business writing.

faq.question: Can it help when I do not know my topic yet?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an idea mining path for users with scattered thoughts and unclear direction.

faq.question: Can it skip idea mining?
faq.answer: Yes. If the thesis is clear, it moves directly to framework discussion and drafting.

faq.question: Does it research facts online?
faq.answer: No. It organizes and drafts from user-provided ideas, examples, and context.

faq.question: How does it keep my voice?
faq.answer: It records user phrases and examples, then uses them when drafting and revising.

faq.question: Can I revise the final article?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes feedback-based revision for structure, tone, examples, and length.
